77	// reVerdictLine matches "### Verdict: APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK"
78	// anywhere in the output, case-insensitive, allowing 1–4 leading hashes.
79	var reVerdictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`)
80	var reReviewSectionHeading = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+(AC Grades|Summary|Findings)\s*:?\s*$`)
81	var reACReference = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\bAC#\s*([0-9]+)\b`)
82	
83	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable is returned by ParseReviewVerdictStrict when
84	// the reviewer output does not contain a recognizable `### Verdict: ...`
85	// line. Callers should surface this as a review-error (retryable) rather
86	// than silently treating it as BLOCK — the pre-ddx-f7ae036f behavior was to
87	// default-to-BLOCK, which produced spurious APPROVE→BLOCK mis-records
88	// whenever the reviewer's output shape drifted (codex stream frames,
89	// rationale-only responses, stall-truncated output).
90	var ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable = fmt.Errorf("reviewer output: no recognizable verdict line")
91	
92	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict is the structured-error variant of
93	// ParseReviewVerdict: on unparseable input it returns
94	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable instead of silently returning VerdictBlock.
95	// The execute-loop uses this path so a parse failure reopens the bead for
96	// retry rather than recording a false BLOCK.
97	func ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output string) (ReviewVerdict, error) {
98		m := reVerdictLine.FindStringSubmatch(output)
99		if m == nil {
100			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
101		}
102		switch strings.ToUpper(strings.TrimSpace(m[1])) {
103		case "APPROVE":
104			return VerdictApprove, nil
105		case "REQUEST_CHANGES":
106			return VerdictRequestChanges, nil
107		case "BLOCK":
108			return VerdictBlock, nil
109		default:
110			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
111		}
112	}
113	
114	// ParseReviewVerdict extracts the verdict from a review agent's output.
115	// The expected format includes a line: ### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
116	//
117	// Deprecated: returns VerdictBlock on unparseable input, which masks
118	// legitimate verdict-parse failures as BLOCKs and caused the wave-2
119	// review-malfunction incident (ddx-f7ae036f). New callers should use
120	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict and treat ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a
121	// retryable review-error.
122	func ParseReviewVerdict(output string) ReviewVerdict {
123		v, err := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output)
124		if err != nil {
125			return VerdictBlock
126		}
127		return v
128	}

130	func ParseReviewResult(output string) (ReviewVerdict, []ReviewAC, string) {
131		verdict := ParseReviewVerdict(output)
132		perAC := parseReviewACTable(output)
133		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(extractReviewRationale(output, perAC))
134		if len(perAC) == 0 {
135			perAC = synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict, rationale)
136		}
137		return verdict, perAC, rationale
138	}

140	func parseReviewACTable(output string) []ReviewAC {
141		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
142		var rows []ReviewAC
143		inTable := false
144		for _, raw := range lines {
145			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
146			if line == "" {
147				if inTable {
148					break
149				}
150				continue
151			}
152			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
153				inTable = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(line), "ac grades")
154				continue
155			}
156			if !inTable || !strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
157				continue
158			}
159			cols := splitMarkdownTableRow(line)
160			if len(cols) < 4 {
161				continue
162			}
163			if cols[0] == "#" || strings.HasPrefix(cols[0], "---") {
164				continue
165			}
166			n, err := strconv.Atoi(cols[0])
167			if err != nil {
168				continue
169			}
170			rows = append(rows, ReviewAC{
171				Number:   n,
172				Item:     cols[1],
173				Grade:    cols[2],
174				Evidence: cols[3],
175			})
176		}
177		return rows
178	}

180	func splitMarkdownTableRow(line string) []string {
181		parts := strings.Split(line, "|")
182		out := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
183		for _, part := range parts {
184			part = strings.TrimSpace(part)
185			if part == "" {
186				continue
187			}
188			out = append(out, part)
189		}
190		return out
191	}

193	func extractReviewRationale(output string, perAC []ReviewAC) string {
194		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
195		var summaryLines []string
196		var findingLines []string
197		var section string
198		for _, raw := range lines {
199			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
200			if line == "" {
201				continue
202			}
203			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "## Review:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "### Verdict:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "#### Verdict:") {
204				continue
205			}
206			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
207				heading := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimLeft(line, "# "), ":")))
208				switch heading {
209				case "summary":
210					section = "summary"
211				case "findings":
212					section = "findings"
213				case "ac grades":
214					section = "ac"
215				default:
216					section = ""
217				}
218				continue
219			}
220			if section == "ac" && strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
221				continue
222			}
223			switch section {
224			case "findings":
225				line = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "-"))
226				if line != "" {
227					findingLines = append(findingLines, line)
228				}
229			case "summary":
230				summaryLines = append(summaryLines, line)
231			}
232		}
233		if len(findingLines) > 0 {
234			return strings.Join(findingLines, "\n")
235		}
236		if len(summaryLines) > 0 {
237			return strings.Join(summaryLines, "\n")
238		}
239		var unmet []string
240		for _, ac := range perAC {
241			if strings.EqualFold(ac.Grade, string(VerdictApprove)) {
242				continue
243			}
244			label := fmt.Sprintf("AC#%d", ac.Number)
245			if ac.Item != "" {
246				label += " " + ac.Item
247			}
248			if ac.Evidence != "" {
249				unmet = append(unmet, label+": "+ac.Evidence)
250			} else {
251				unmet = append(unmet, label)
252			}
253		}
254		return strings.Join(unmet, "\n")
255	}

257	func synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict ReviewVerdict, rationale string) []ReviewAC {
258		if rationale == "" {
259			return nil
260		}
261		matches := reACReference.FindAllStringSubmatch(rationale, -1)
262		if len(matches) == 0 {
263			return nil
264		}
265		seen := make(map[int]struct{}, len(matches))
266		out := make([]ReviewAC, 0, len(matches))
267		for _, match := range matches {
268			if len(match) < 2 {
269				continue
270			}
271			n, err := strconv.Atoi(match[1])
272			if err != nil {
273				continue
274			}
275			if _, ok := seen[n]; ok {
276				continue
277			}
278			seen[n] = struct{}{}
279			out = append(out, ReviewAC{
280				Number:   n,
281				Grade:    string(verdict),
282				Evidence: rationale,
283			})
284		}
285		return out
286	}

322	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
323	// The reviewing agent must produce APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK with
324	// the exact markdown format described below.
325	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
326	
327	## Your task
328	
329	Examine the diff and each acceptance-criteria (AC) item. For each item assign one grade:
330	
331	- **APPROVE** — fully and correctly implemented; cite the specific file path and line that proves it.
332	- **REQUEST_CHANGES** — partially implemented or has fixable minor issues.
333	- **BLOCK** — not implemented, incorrectly implemented, or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
334	
335	Overall verdict rule:
336	- All items APPROVE → **APPROVE**
337	- Any item BLOCK → **BLOCK**
338	- Otherwise → **REQUEST_CHANGES**
339	
340	## Required output format
341	
342	Respond with a structured review using exactly this layout (replace placeholder text):
343	
344	---
345	## Review: <bead-id> iter <N>
346	
347	### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
348	
349	### AC Grades
350	
351	| # | Item | Grade | Evidence |
352	|---|------|-------|----------|
353	| 1 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | APPROVE | path/to/file.go:42 — brief note |
354	| 2 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | BLOCK   | — not found in diff |
355	
356	### Summary
357	
358	<1–3 sentences on overall implementation quality and any recurring theme in findings.>
359	
360	### Findings
361	
362	<Bullet list of REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK findings. Each finding must name the specific file, function, or test that is missing or wrong — specific enough for the next agent to act on without re-reading the entire diff. Omit this section entirely if verdict is APPROVE.>`
363	
364	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
365	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
366	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
367	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
368		Caps evidence.Caps
369	}
370	
371	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
372	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
373	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
374	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
375	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
376		Prompt   string
377		Overflow bool
378		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
379	}
380	
381	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
382	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
383	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
384	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
385		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
386	}
387	
388	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
389	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
390	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
391	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
392	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
393	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
394	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
395	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
396		caps := opts.Caps
397		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
398			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
399		}
400	
401		var sb strings.Builder
402		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
403	
404		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
405	
406		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
407		beadStart := sb.Len()
408		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
409		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
410	
411		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
412			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
413		} else {
414			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
415		}
416	
417		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
418			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
419		} else {
420			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
421		}
422	
423		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
424			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
425		}
426	
427		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
428			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
429		}
430	
431		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
432		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
433			Name:          "bead",
434			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
435			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
436		})
437	
438		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
439		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
440		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
441			invStart := sb.Len()
442			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
443			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
444			for _, f := range allFiles {
445				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
446				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
447			}
448			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
449			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
450				Name:          "changed-files",
451				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
452				SelectedItems: paths,
453			})
454		}
455	
456		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
457		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
458		if len(refs) == 0 {
459			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
460		} else {
461			for _, ref := range refs {
462				if ref.Title != "" {
463					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
464				} else {
465					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
466				}
467				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
468				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
469				if readErr != nil {
470					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
471					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
472						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
473						TruncationReason: "read_error",
474						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
475					})
476				} else {
477					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
478					if truncated {
479						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
480					}
481					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", txt)
482					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
483						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
484						BytesIncluded: len(content),
485						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
486					}
487					if truncated {
488						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
489						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
490					}
491					sections = append(sections, sec)
492				}
493				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
494			}
495		}
496		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
497	
498		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
499		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
500		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
501		diffSection.Name = "diff"
502		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
503		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n"))
504	
505		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
506		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
507		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
508		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
509	
510		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
511	
512		out := sb.String()
513		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
514			Prompt:   out,
515			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
516			Sections: sections,
517		}
518	}
519	
520	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
521	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
522	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
523	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
524	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
525	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
526	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
527		if len(files) == 0 {
528			return originalDiff
529		}
530		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
531			if f.Path == "" {
532				return 3
533			}
534			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
535				return 0
536			}
537			for _, r := range refs {
538				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
539					return 1
540				}
541			}
542			return 2
543		}
544		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
545		copy(ordered, files)
546		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
547	
548		var b2 strings.Builder
549		for _, f := range ordered {
550			body := f.Body
551			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
552				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
553			}
554			b2.WriteString(body)
555		}
556		return b2.String()
557	}

559	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
560	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
561	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
562	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
563	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
564		var b strings.Builder
565		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
566		b.WriteString(f.Path)
567		b.WriteString(" b/")
568		b.WriteString(f.Path)
569		b.WriteString("\n")
570		if f.Stat != "" {
571			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
572			b.WriteString("\n")
573		}
574		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
575			b.WriteString(h)
576			b.WriteString("\n")
577		}
578		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
579		return b.String()
580	}

582	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
583	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
584	// exact symbolic resolution).
585	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
586		if text == "" || p == "" {
587			return false
588		}
589		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
590			return true
591		}
592		base := path.Base(p)
593		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
594	}
595	
596	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
597	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
598	// documents.
599	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
600		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
601			return false
602		}
603		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
604		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
605			return true
606		}
607		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
608			return true
609		}
610		return false
611	}

613	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
614	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
615		var buf bytes.Buffer
616		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
617			"&", "&",
618			"<", "<",
619			">", ">",
620		).Replace(s))
621		return buf.String()
622	}

624	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
625	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
626	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
627	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
628		ProjectRoot string
629		BeadStore   BeadReader
630		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
631		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
632		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
633		Service agentlib.DdxAgent
634		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
635		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
636		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
637		Runner AgentRunner
638		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
639		// When empty, Harness defaults to "claude" and Model is resolved
640		// from TierSmart for the chosen harness.
641		Harness string
642		Model   string
643		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
644		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
645		// applies.
646		Caps evidence.Caps
647	}
648	
649	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
650	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev, implHarness, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
651		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
652		if err != nil {
653			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
654		}
655	
656		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
657		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
658		if err != nil {
659			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
660		}
661	
662		// Resolve governing document references.
663		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
664	
665		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
666		iter := 1
667	
668		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
669		caps := r.Caps
670		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
671			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
672		}
673		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
674		prompt := built.Prompt
675		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
676		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
677		if err != nil {
678			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
679		}
680		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
681			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
682		}
683	
684		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
685		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
686		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
687		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
688		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
689		if built.Overflow {
690			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
691			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
692				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
693				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
694				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
695				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
696				ReviewerModel:   r.Model,
697				BaseRev:         baseRev,
698				ResultRev:       resultRev,
699				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
700			}
701			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
702				Harness:      r.Harness,
703				Model:        r.Model,
704				BaseRev:      baseRev,
705				ResultRev:    resultRev,
706				Verdict:      string(VerdictBlock),
707				BeadID:       beadID,
708				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
709			}, reviewArtifactResult{
710				Verdict: string(VerdictBlock),
711				Error:   evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
712			})
713			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
714				evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow, len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
715		}
716	
717		// Resolve reviewer harness and model.
718		reviewHarness := r.Harness
719		if reviewHarness == "" {
720			if implHarness != "" {
721				reviewHarness = implHarness
722			} else {
723				reviewHarness = "claude" // default reviewer harness
724			}
725		}
726		reviewModel := r.Model
727		if reviewModel == "" {
728			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
729		}
730	
731		start := time.Now()
732		runOpts := RunOptions{
733			Context: ctx,
734			Harness: reviewHarness,
735			Model:   reviewModel,
736			Prompt:  prompt,
737			WorkDir: r.ProjectRoot,
738		}
739		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runOpts)
740	
741		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
742		if runErr != nil {
743			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
744				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
745				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
746				Error:           runErr.Error(),
747				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
748				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
749				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
750				ResultRev:       resultRev,
751				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
752				DurationMS:      durationMS,
753			}
754			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
755				Harness:      reviewHarness,
756				Model:        reviewModel,
757				BaseRev:      reviewRes.BaseRev,
758				ResultRev:    resultRev,
759				Verdict:      string(reviewRes.Verdict),
760				BeadID:       beadID,
761				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
762			}, reviewArtifactResult{
763				Verdict:   string(reviewRes.Verdict),
764				Rationale: reviewRes.Rationale,
765				Error:     reviewRes.Error,
766			})
767			return reviewRes, nil
768		}
769	
770		actualHarness := reviewHarness
771		actualModel := reviewModel
772		if result != nil {
773			if result.Harness != "" {
774				actualHarness = result.Harness
775			}
776			if result.Model != "" {
777				actualModel = result.Model
778			}
779			durationMS = result.DurationMS
780		}
781	
782		output := ""
783		sessionID := ""
784		if result != nil {
785			output = result.Output
786			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
787		}
788		// Strict parse: unparseable reviewer output surfaces as a typed error so
789		// the execute-loop can classify it as review-error (retryable) rather
790		// than mis-recording it as BLOCK (ddx-f7ae036f). The review result
791		// artifacts still land on disk for forensics via writeReviewArtifacts
792		// below, regardless of the error path.
793		strictVerdict, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output)
794		perAC := parseReviewACTable(output)
795		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(extractReviewRationale(output, perAC))
796		if len(perAC) == 0 {
797			perAC = synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(strictVerdict, rationale)
798		}
799		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
800		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
801			Verdict:         strictVerdict,
802			Rationale:       rationale,
803			PerAC:           perAC,
804			RawOutput:       output,
805			ReviewerHarness: actualHarness,
806			ReviewerModel:   actualModel,
807			SessionID:       sessionID,
808			BaseRev:         baseRev,
809			ResultRev:       resultRev,
810			ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
811			DurationMS:      durationMS,
812		}
813		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
814			Harness:      actualHarness,
815			Model:        actualModel,
816			SessionID:    sessionID,
817			BaseRev:      baseRev,
818			ResultRev:    resultRev,
819			Verdict:      string(strictVerdict),
820			BeadID:       beadID,
821			ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
822		}, reviewArtifactResult{
823			Verdict:   string(strictVerdict),
824			PerAC:     perAC,
825			Rationale: rationale,
826			Error:     reviewRes.Error,
827		})
828		if parseErr != nil {
829			// Return the review result alongside the parse error so the caller
830			// can surface the full rationale + raw output as review-error event
831			// body (loop's reviewErr path) while still having access to the
832			// reviewer text for operator forensics.
833			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
834		}
835		return reviewRes, nil
836	}

838	// dispatchReviewRun resolves how the review invocation should be executed.
839	// Resolution order matches dispatchAgentRun:
840	//  1. r.Runner (test injection seam) — used directly via Runner.Run.
841	//  2. r.Service (pre-built service) — used via RunViaServiceWith.
842	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(ProjectRoot)
843	//     and dispatch via RunViaServiceWith.
844	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runOpts RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
845		if r.Runner != nil {
846			return r.Runner.Run(runOpts)
847		}
848		svc := r.Service
849		if svc == nil {
850			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(r.ProjectRoot)
851			if err != nil {
852				return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: build agent service: %w", err)
853			}
854			svc = built
855		}
856		return RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, r.ProjectRoot, runOpts)
857	}

859	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
860	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
861	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
862	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
863	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
864		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
865		defer cancel()
866		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
867		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
868		if err != nil {
869			return "", err
870		}
871		return string(out), nil
872	}

874	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
875		if resultRev == "" {
876			return ""
877		}
878		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
879		if err != nil {
880			return ""
881		}
882		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
883	}

885	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
886		if artifacts == nil {
887			return nil
888		}
889		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ManifestAbs, manifest); err != nil {
890			return err
891		}
892		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
893	}

895	func ReadReviewArtifactResult(path string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
896		//evidence:allow-unbounded — review result artifacts are small JSON
897		// documents written by writeReviewArtifacts; bounded by construction.
898		raw, err := os.ReadFile(path)
899		if err != nil {
900			return nil, err
901		}
902		var artifact reviewArtifactResult
903		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &artifact); err != nil {
904			return nil, err
905		}
906		return &ReviewResult{
907			Verdict:   ReviewVerdict(artifact.Verdict),
908			Rationale: artifact.Rationale,
909			PerAC:     artifact.PerAC,
910			Error:     artifact.Error,
911		}, nil
912	}

215		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
216		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
217		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
218		Now                 func() time.Time
219		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
220		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
221		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
222		Reviewer BeadReviewer
223	}

225	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to opts.ProgressCh non-blocking.
226	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
227	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
228		if ch == nil {
229			return
230		}
231		select {
232		case ch <- evt:
233		default:
234		}
235	}

239		return ProgressEvent{
240			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
241			AttemptID: attemptID,
242			WorkerID:  workerID,
243			ProjectID: projectID,
244			BeadID:    beadID,
245			Harness:   opts.Harness,
246			Model:     opts.Model,
247			Profile:   opts.Profile,
248			Phase:     phase,

250			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
251			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),

253		}

260	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
261		if w.Store == nil {
262			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
263		}
264		if w.Executor == nil {
265			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
266		}
267	
268		now := w.Now
269		if now == nil {
270			now = time.Now
271		}
272		assignee := opts.Assignee
273		if assignee == "" {
274			assignee = "ddx"
275		}
276		noProgressCooldown := opts.NoProgressCooldown
277		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
278			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
279		}
280		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
281		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
282			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
283		}
284		heartbeatInterval := opts.HeartbeatInterval
285		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
286			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
287		}
288	
289		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
290		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
291	
292		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
293			writeLoopEvent(opts.EventSink, opts.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
294		}
295	
296		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
297			"worker_id":    opts.WorkerID,
298			"project_root": opts.ProjectRoot,
299			"harness":      opts.Harness,
300			"model":        opts.Model,
301			"session_id":   opts.SessionID,
302			"assignee":     assignee,
303			"once":         opts.Once,
304		})
305		defer func() {
306			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
307				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
308				"successes":           result.Successes,
309				"failures":            result.Failures,
310				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
311			})
312		}()
313	
314		for {
315			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
316			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
317			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
318			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
319			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
320				return result, err
321			}
322	
323			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, opts.LabelFilter)
324			if err != nil {
325				return result, err
326			}
327			if !ok {
328				if result.Attempts == 0 {
329					result.NoReadyWork = true
330					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
331						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
332							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
333								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
334								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
335								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
336								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
337								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
338							}
339						}
340					}
341				}
342				if opts.PollInterval <= 0 {
343					return result, nil
344				}
345				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, opts.PollInterval); err != nil {
346					return result, err
347				}
348				continue
349			}
350	
351			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
352	
353			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
354			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
355			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
356			if opts.PreClaimHook != nil {
357				if hookErr := opts.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
358					if opts.Log != nil {
359						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
360					}
361					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
362						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
363						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
364					})
365					continue
366				}
367			}
368	
369			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
370				continue
371			}
372	
373			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
374				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
375				"title":    candidate.Title,
376				"assignee": assignee,
377			})
378	
379			if opts.Log != nil {
380				if candidate.Title != "" {
381					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
382				} else {
383					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
384				}
385			}
386	
387			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
388			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
389			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
390			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
391			runStart := now()
392			phaseSeq := 0
393			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
394				phaseSeq++
395				emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
396					opts.WorkerID, opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
397					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(), opts,
398				))
399			}
400	
401			nextPhase("queueing", false)
402	
403			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
404			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
405			hbWG.Add(1)
406			go func(beadID string) {
407				defer hbWG.Done()
408				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
409				defer ticker.Stop()
410				for {
411					select {
412					case <-hbCtx.Done():
413						return
414					case <-ticker.C:
415						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
416					}
417				}
418			}(candidate.ID)
419	
420			nextPhase("running", false)
421	
422			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
423			hbCancel()
424			hbWG.Wait()
425			if err != nil {
426				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
427					BeadID: candidate.ID,
428					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
429					Detail: err.Error(),
430				}
431			}
432			if report.BeadID == "" {
433				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
434			}
435			if report.Status == "" {
436				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
437			}
438			if report.Detail == "" {
439				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
440			}
441	
442			result.Attempts++
443	
444			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
445				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
446				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
447				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
448				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
449				reviewApproved := true
450				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || opts.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
451	
452				if reviewSkipped {
453					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
454						return result, err
455					}
456				}
457	
458				if w.Reviewer != nil && !opts.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
459					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
460					if reviewErr != nil {
461						// Review error: log event but leave bead closed (don't block on reviewer failures).
462						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
463							Kind:      "review-error",
464							Summary:   "review agent error",
465							Body:      reviewErr.Error(),
466							Actor:     assignee,
467							Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
468							CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
469						})
470					} else {
471						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
472						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
473						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
474						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
475						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
476						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
477						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
478						// log if the artifact write failed.
479						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
480						if artifactErr != nil && opts.Log != nil {
481							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
482						}
483	
484						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
485						case VerdictApprove:
486							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
487							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
488							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
489							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
490								Kind:      "review",
491								Summary:   "APPROVE",
492								Body:      reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath),
493								Actor:     assignee,
494								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
495								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
496							})
497							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
498								return result, cerr
499							}
500						case VerdictRequestChanges:
501							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
502							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
503							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
504							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
505							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
506								Kind:      "review",
507								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
508								Body:      reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath),
509								Actor:     assignee,
510								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
511								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
512							})
513							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
514							if reopenNotes == "" {
515								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
516							}
517							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
518								return result, reopenErr
519							}
520							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
521							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
522							reviewApproved = false
523						case VerdictBlock:
524							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
525							if rationale == "" {
526								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
527									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
528									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
529									Body:      reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath),
530									Actor:     assignee,
531									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
532									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
533								})
534								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
535								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
536								report.ReviewRationale = ""
537								reviewApproved = false
538								break
539							}
540							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
541							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
542							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
543								Kind:      "review",
544								Summary:   "BLOCK",
545								Body:      rationale,
546								Actor:     assignee,
547								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
548								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
549							})
550							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
551							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
552								return result, reopenErr
553							}
554							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
555							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
556							reviewApproved = false
557						}
558					}
559				}
560	
561				if reviewApproved {
562					result.Successes++
563					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
564				} else {
565					result.Failures++
566					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
567				}
568			} else {
569				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
570					return result, err
571				}
572				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
573					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
574					if cerr != nil {
575						return result, cerr
576					}
577					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale)
578					if aerr != nil {
579						return result, aerr
580					}
581					if satisfied {
582						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
583						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
584						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
585						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
586						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
587						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
588						// satisfied beads).
589						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
590						if evidence != "" {
591							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
592							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
593							report.Detail = evidence
594						}
595						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
596						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
597							return result, cerr
598						}
599						result.Successes++
600						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
601					} else {
602						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
603						// move on to other beads.
604						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
605							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
606							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
607								return result, cerr
608							}
609							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
610						}
611						result.Failures++
612						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
613					}
614				} else {
615					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
616						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
617						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
618							return result, err
619						}
620						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
621					}
622					result.Failures++
623					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
624				}
625			}
626	
627			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
628	
629			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
630			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
631			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
632			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
633			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
634				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
635					return result, err
636				}
637			}
638	
639			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
640			terminalPhase := "failed"
641			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
642				terminalPhase = "done"
643			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
644				terminalPhase = "preserved"
645			}
646			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
647			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
648			if finalAttemptID == "" {
649				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
650			}
651			phaseSeq++
652			emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
653				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
654				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
655				WorkerID:  opts.WorkerID,
656				ProjectID: opts.ProjectRoot,
657				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
658				Harness:   opts.Harness,
659				Model:     opts.Model,
660				Profile:   opts.Profile,
661				Phase:     terminalPhase,
662				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
663				Heartbeat: false,
664				TS:        now().UTC(),
665				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
666				Message:   report.Detail,
667			})
668	
669			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
670				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
671				"status":               report.Status,
672				"detail":               report.Detail,
673				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
674				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
675				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
676				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
677				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
678				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
679			})
680	
681			if opts.Log != nil {
682				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
683			}
684	
685			if opts.Once {
686				return result, nil
687			}
688		}
689	}

691	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
692		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
693		if err != nil {
694			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
695		}
696		for _, candidate := range ready {
697			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
698				continue
699			}
700			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
701				continue
702			}
703			return candidate, true, nil
704		}
705		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
706	}

708	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
709	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
710	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
711	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
712	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
713		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
714			return
715		}
716		provider := report.Provider
717		if provider == "" {
718			provider = report.Harness
719		}
720		if provider == "" {
721			return
722		}
723		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
724			"resolved_provider": provider,
725			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
726			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
727			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
728			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
729			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
730			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
731			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
732		})
733		if err != nil {
734			return
735		}
736		summary := "provider=" + provider
737		if report.Model != "" {
738			summary += " model=" + report.Model
739		}
740		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
741			Kind:      "routing",
742			Summary:   summary,
743			Body:      string(body),
744			Actor:     "ddx",
745			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
746			CreatedAt: createdAt,
747		})
748	}
749	
750	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
751		parts := []string{}
752		if report.Detail != "" {
753			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
754		}
755		if report.Tier != "" {
756			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
757		}
758		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
759			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
760		}
761		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
762			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
763		}
764		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
765			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
766		}
767		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
768			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
769		}
770		if report.ResultRev != "" {
771			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
772		}
773		if report.BaseRev != "" {
774			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
775		}
776		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
777			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
778		}
779	
780		return bead.BeadEvent{
781			Kind:      "execute-bead",
782			Summary:   report.Status,
783			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
784			Actor:     actor,
785			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
786			CreatedAt: createdAt,
787		}
788	}

790	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
791	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
792	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
793	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
794	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
795	// never break the core execute-loop.
796	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
797		if sink == nil {
798			return
799		}
800		if data == nil {
801			data = map[string]any{}
802		}
803		entry := map[string]any{
804			"session_id": sessionID,
805			"type":       eventType,
806			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
807			"data":       data,
808		}
809		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
810		if err != nil {
811			return
812		}
813		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
814		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
815	}
816	
817	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
818	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
819	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
820		switch report.Status {
821		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
822			shortRev := report.ResultRev
823			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
824				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
825			}
826			if shortRev != "" {
827				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
828			}
829			return "merged"
830		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
831			return "already_satisfied"
832		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
833			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
834				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
835			}
836			return "no_changes"
837		default:
838			detail := report.Detail
839			if detail == "" {
840				detail = report.Status
841			}
842			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
843				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
844			}
845			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
846		}
847	}
848	
849	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
850	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
851	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
852	
853	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
854	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
855	
856	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
857	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
858	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
859	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
860	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
861		if rationale == "" {
862			return false
863		}
864		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
865	}
866	
867	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
868	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
869	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
870	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
871	//
872	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
873	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
874	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
875	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
876	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale string) (bool, string, error) {
877		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
878			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
879		}
880		if rationaleIsSpecific(rationale) {
881			evidence := rationale
882			return true, evidence, nil
883		}
884		if noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose {
885			return true, fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount), nil
886		}
887		return false, "", nil
888	}
889	
890	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
891		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
892			return false
893		}
894		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
895	}
896	
897	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
898		if d <= 0 {
899			return nil
900		}
901		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
902		defer timer.Stop()
903		select {
904		case <-ctx.Done():
905			return ctx.Err()
906		case <-timer.C:
907			return nil
908		}
909	}

17	// is the silent-false-closure surface that 738edf47 eliminates.
18	func TestExecuteBeadWorker_ReviewerFailureModesKeepBeadOpen(t *testing.T) {

22		}{

26					return nil, errors.New("reviewer harness exited with code 1")
27				}),

31				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
32					return &ReviewResult{
33						Verdict:   "",
34						RawOutput: "",
35					}, nil
36				}),

39				name: "reviewer output unparseable — no recognizable verdict line",
40				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
41					return &ReviewResult{

43						RawOutput: "Reviewer crashed mid-stream with no structured verdict.",
44					}, nil
45				}),
